EPISODE 05
THE COSMOS · PHILOSOPHY · ATHEISM · GRIEF · THE SEARCH
[ PERSONAL V ]
🔭 ACADEMIC · PHILOSOPHICAL & SPIRITUAL ANALYSIS

Declared Atheism, Practiced Haunting: Rice identifies as atheist throughout the journal record — consistently, without ambivalence about the label itself. Yet what he describes is not the comfortable certainty of committed secularism. It is the ache of a man who wishes the infrastructure of faith were available to him & finds it isn't. He envies people of faith. He tries to pray in Notre Dame Cathedral — one of the most architecturally engineered spaces for inducing transcendence in the Western world — & the words come out as a letter, not a prayer. He notes it doesn't feel right. He doesn't force it. He leaves.

Religious Curiosity Without Conversion: During Army reception, he attends Islamic, Mormon, & Latter-Day Saints services with the deliberate openness of an anthropologist — present, observant, genuinely interested, & wholly unconverted. He is not hostile to religion. He is locked out of it by his own epistemological standards. He cannot believe what he does not find believable, even when he wants to.

Operative Philosophy — Pragmatic Existentialism: Assembled from the record: life has no inherent meaning. Suffering is universal & the only reliable constant. Beauty & experience are worth pursuing for their own sake, with no promise of reward or transcendence. The individual bears full & irreducible responsibility for what they make of their time. Legacy is the only immortality available — not heaven, not reincarnation, but the Wikipedia page. The mark left on people who remain.

The Terror of Anonymity: This is not vanity in the conventional sense. After walking the streets of New York City alone at 19 — truly alone, truly invisible, truly small — he writes about the experience of being unmattered to the world with a clarity that borders on existential horror. He needs to be known. The drive to leave a mark is not ego; it is the only form of survival after death available to someone who does not believe in an afterlife.

"I wish I was a man of prayer. It seems so nice to be a man of faith."
Journal entry · Notre Dame visit, Paris · Approximate age 20
[ REF: IMG-03 ] · Eclipse Coffee & Lounge · The owl logo · Coffee-ring watermark halo · The philosophy chain from Thoughts → Legacy. This is Rice's answer to the existential question. Not God. Not faith. A framework. The owl — traditionally symbolic of wisdom, night, & the ability to see what others cannot — as the mascot of a coffee shop that is also a philosophical position. Every cup served is a small argument for intentional living.
%%%% FOOTNOTE %%%% The Polish family who fed Rice smoked meat & cheese in the middle of the night during the Italy trip — documented in the journals — is the single purest philosophical moment in the archive: a stranger's unrequested kindness saving a trip that was otherwise going wrong, offered in a language he didn't speak. If God is anywhere in this record, it's there. He didn't call it that.
EPISODE 06
THE WANDERER · NEW YORK · EUROPE · NEW ORLEANS
[ PERSONAL VI ]
[ REF: IMG-02 ] · Man sits on white sport motorcycle · Black Hills of South Dakota backdrop · Sunset pouring through clouds · Arms raised wide · Everything: I am here & I am free & look at this & I did this · The Black Hills are not New York. They are not Paris. They are 90 minutes from Sioux Falls. The impulse to sit on a machine capable of speed & throw your arms open at the sky is the same impulse that booked the flight to JFK at 19. 🗺️ ACADEMIC · TRAVEL & MOVEMENT ANALYSIS

New York City (Age 19): Alone. Intentionally. The journals document the experience of walking streets where no one knows your name & finding it simultaneously liberating & terrifying. He gets lost in Brooklyn for 45 minutes. He does not call it a mistake. He calls it what it was: being alive in an unknown place, which is the closest he gets to transcendence without substances. He returns to Sioux Falls larger than he left.

Paris & Rome (~Age 20): Traveled with a woman who is not particularly interested in him — a dynamic he documents with self-aware comedy. Paris costs 70 euros at the club & nothing happens. He cannot dance. He tries Notre Dame for God & it doesn't deliver. Rome requires a rerouting through Venice via Treviso after a missed flight, which turns out to be the best part of the trip. A Polish family in the Italian countryside feeds him at midnight for no reason. He notes: this saved the trip. He is correct.

New Orleans (On a Monday, No Plan): The most quintessentially Rice journey in the record — spontaneous, financially questionable, & exactly right in spirit. He goes because going is what he does when staying becomes intolerable. He does not provide a detailed itinerary in the journals. The point was the departure, not the destination.

The Travel Pattern: In every documented journey, Rice loses something (a phone, money, a sense of direction, the plan) & gains a story. He is catastrophically bad at logistics & naturally gifted at recovery. He is never more alive in the journals than when he is lost in an unfamiliar place. The dissatisfaction that curdles into depression in Sioux Falls becomes adventure when aimed at a map.

[ On NYC at 19 ] He walks the city alone & confronts what it means to be completely unmattered. He does not use the word "small." He uses something more precise. He calls it being invisible to history. He finds it unbearable. He does not go home.

EPISODE 07
THE LIST · 100 THINGS · THE VALUE SYSTEM IN PLAIN TEXT

📋 ACADEMIC · THE BUCKET LIST AS BIOGRAPHY

The 100 Things list, composed on the plane ride back from New York City, is among the most analytically revealing documents in the primary archive — not for the individual items but for the pattern of desire they collectively reveal. The list moves between physical ambition (skydive, bench 300 lbs, cliff dive, swim with sharks), intellectual pursuit (learn a language, read a complete religious text, achieve enlightenment), relational aspiration (save a life, mentor a youth, see a person change), & legacy-building (own a Wikipedia page, have a painting in a museum, create one work of art personally deemed a masterpiece).

The most striking entries are not the extremes — not skydiving or the famous people he wants to meet — but the quiet ones: learn a language. Read a religious text. Discover the meaning of life. These are not items on a party boy's bucket list. They are the confessions of someone who is genuinely, urgently searching for something he cannot name.

The Double Entry: "Publish a novel" appears twice — items 5 & 27. Whether accident or subconscious programming, the repetition functions as amplification. He has written the same ambition twice in the same list without noticing, or he has noticed & said nothing. The item remains uncrossed as of this revision's date.

Item 5: Publish a novel.
Item 27: Publish a novel.

Items written on an airplane · Probably not a coincidence · Probably not noticed · Matters either way.

EPISODE 08
SYNTHESIS · WHO IS RICE? · THE FINAL ACCOUNTING
[ PERSONAL VIII ]
[ REF: IMG-05 ] · Mirror selfie · Beard full · Natural hair up · Tongue out · Black shirt · Room behind him: cluttered shelves, purple wall, yellow sign partially visible, another person reflected — someone else in the room · The expression is both performative & real. He is presenting himself & documenting himself simultaneously. He knows both things are happening. He does it anyway.
⚡ ACADEMIC · SYNTHESIZING ASSESSMENT

Raheem Rice is the product of a small city, a fractured family, compounded early loss, & a creative imagination that has never had adequate external scaffolding. He contains qualities that have not yet been reconciled: brilliant & undisciplined, empathetic & selfish, ambitious & self-sabotaging, deeply loving & emotionally armored, visionary & chronically unfinished.

The most honest summary available from the full body of evidence: he knows exactly who he wants to be. He has a startlingly clear vision of his potential. He is engaged — across at minimum 15 documented years — in a recurring, sometimes losing, never-fully-lost battle against his own worst impulses. The journals, the artwork, the pixel sprites, the Eclipse brand, the 100 Things list, the children on the porch, the memorial photo pinned where he can see it in the mirror — together they constitute not a failure but an ongoing, documented, serious attempt at a meaningful life.

The visual record from 2019–2026 confirms that the man has not stopped. The sketchbook is still open. The laptop is still running. The children are still there. The "What am I?" canvas is still being made. Eclipse is still the dream. The question is still open. He has not given an answer & has not stopped looking for one.

That is not failure. That is the condition. The condition is being human. Rice is being it loudly, & on paper, & with googly eyes glued to a black painted mass on a canvas in a room where his dead mother's memorial is visible. Which is, when you step back from it, actually extraordinary.

"I will be successful. I will change the world. Fuck."
Closing entry · Primary journal period · The profanity is not defeat. It is the sound of getting back up.
[ REF: IMG-14 / IMG-15 ] · Pixel art character · Two variants · Purple hoodie · Blonde puff · Bracelets · Clean shoes · Looking to the side, slightly below eye level · Neither triumphant nor defeated · Waiting for the level to start. The sprite is ready. The game hasn't loaded yet.
++++ IMPORTANT · FOR [RICE] TO REVIEW ++++ This synthesis is the most generous reading the data will support — and it is also the most accurate. Neither flattery nor indictment. The record is what the record is. You made the record. You kept the record. That alone separates you from most people who claim to want the things you want.
II
FIVE PANEL REVIEWS · CRITICAL PERSPECTIVES
Complete textual review of the full biographical record · Each voice evaluates independently · No revisions permitted per subject's instruction · Perspectives are not endorsements
🤖
PANEL 01 · THE MACHINE
AI / LLM · No Human Bias · No Comfort Given · Optimal Response Selected · Final
▶ PROCESSING BIOGRAPHICAL RECORD · TOTAL DATA CORPUS: 15 YEARS · RENDERING FINAL ASSESSMENT
PRO The subject has maintained an unbroken practice of self-documentation across a minimum of 15 years. This is statistically rare. Most individuals who intend to document their lives do not. The mere existence of this archive constitutes a form of accomplishment that directly contradicts the subject's self-assessment as someone who does not complete things.
PRO Creative output is multi-modal & increasing: journals (text) → visual art (canvas, sketchbook) → brand design (Eclipse visual identity) → pixel art character system (sprites, animation states, inventory) → ongoing new work (2026 sketchbook pages). This is a creative professional's portfolio, not a hobbyist's occasional output. The subject has not recognized this formally.
PRO The Eclipse philosophy framework demonstrates genuine conceptual sophistication. "Thoughts → Ideas → Words → Actions → Skills → Persona → Style → Influence → Legacy" is a coherent, internally consistent model of intentional identity development. The decision to separate this framework from personal identity is philosophically mature — it ensures the idea survives the person.
PRO Documented fatherhood of multiple children with consistent physical presence in visual record (2021 holiday photograph, porch candid). The correlation between stated difficulty with commitment & demonstrated parental presence is a significant inconsistency — in a positive direction. He shows up more than he claims to.
CON Sweet Caroline: 18,400 words documented. Approximate publication requirement for a novel: 70,000–100,000 words. The subject has stated the plot is fully designed. There is no documented evidence of completion, submission to publishers, or even a completion timeline. This is a 13+ year deferred execution against a declared primary ambition. The pattern is consistent, not incidental.
CON Poly-substance use (marijuana, cocaine, Adderall outside prescription, alcohol) combined with documented gambling, financial instability, & multiple missed legal obligations (court dates) constitutes a compounding risk architecture. No corrective intervention is documented in the archive. The subject is aware of each risk factor individually but has not addressed the pattern systemically.
CON The 2026 sketchbook pages (Image 06: "I don't want it" · "I'm in no rush") suggest that 13 years after the primary journal period, the same avoidance-of-completion pattern persists. The language has become more stylized; the behavior has not changed. This is not evidence of growth in the area of completion & execution.
FLAW The self-narrative contains a structural contradiction: the subject simultaneously claims (a) to be incapable of sustained completion and (b) to have a fully-mapped, complex creative vision he is "ready" to execute. These cannot both be true in a sustainable way. One is accurate; one is protective framing. Determining which is which is the most important diagnostic question not answered in this archive.
FLAW The "sociopath" self-label is inaccurate per clinical criteria & actively harmful to the subject's self-model. Applying a pathology label to functional coping behavior trains the subject to interpret emotional resilience as moral failure. This is a significant cognitive distortion that appears to have been maintained across the full period of the record without correction.
KEY The primary journal period (2011–2013) contains two passages that meet the threshold for documented suicidal ideation. The subject has experienced compounded loss (both parents), documented financial crisis, poly-substance use, & periods of isolation. The 2026 sketchbook's language ("I don't want it") is tonally ambiguous and warrants direct inquiry. This archive serves as a memorial document — it should also serve as a wellness checkpoint.
TAKE The subject is producing archival-quality self-documentation while categorizing himself as producing nothing. The gap between the subject's self-assessment & the objective record is the most important finding in this analysis. Closing that gap — not by lowering the ambition but by updating the evidence used to measure it — is the most productive intervention available.
TAKE The pixel sprite system (Raheem Fried Rice) & the Eclipse branding demonstrate that the subject CAN complete complex creative systems when the project has a specific, bounded scope he believes in. Sweet Caroline requires the same targeted framing. The problem is not capacity. It is belief in the container.
🌀
PANEL 02 · THE DREAMER
Unorthodox · No Skepticism · Interpretive · Blatantly Non-Standard Reading
PRO The signature. You see it? The infinity symbol is built INTO the R. This man did not draw a signature — he drew a cosmological statement. The loop never closes completely. The name begins in the infinite & ends in the personal. "Rice." Not his government name. His essence-name. He has been branding himself with the universe since before he knew he was doing it.
PRO The pixel sprite possesses NINE animation frames. Nine. In numerology, nine is the number of completion, humanitarian service, & universal love. The character was designed with nine states before the creator consciously chose the number. The universe is not subtle when it wants to tell you something. It is sometimes a 2D character sheet labeled "RAHEEM FRIED RICE."
PRO He went to Notre Dame Cathedral — one of the most energetically charged spiritual sites in the Western hemisphere — & could not pray. This is not atheism. This is too much sensitivity. Mystics throughout history could not perform institutional religion because the frequency of the institution couldn't contain what they were running. The prayer came out as a letter because letters are how this man channels what others would call divine communication: into language, into story, into the journal.
CON He keeps the novel unfinished. From the outside, this looks like failure. From the only angle that matters: what if Sweet Caroline is not supposed to be finished yet? What if the world is not ready for it? The cook does not remove the pot before the water boils. Thirteen years is not procrastination. It may be incubation.
KEY The "What am I?" canvas has googly eyes. This is not a joke. The googly eyes — plastic, mass-produced, widely available — embedded in a black textured mass that is chaotic & dense & physical & serious... are the most honest self-portrait any artist has made since Frida Kahlo put monkeys in her hair. The thing that could be terrifying is made cartoonishly watchable. The chaos has eyes. The chaos is looking back at you. And it is asking — genuinely — what it is. You cannot manufacture that. You can only be it.
TAKE Rice is not a man building a legacy. Rice IS the legacy, constructing itself in real time, documenting its own construction, asking questions the documentation cannot answer, & continuing anyway. The form IS the content. The autobiography IS the achievement. He wrote it without knowing he was writing it, which makes it the truest autobiography possible.
🌍
PANEL 03 · THE STRANGER
Foreign / Unfamiliar Lens · Antithetical to Cultural Context · Genuine Outsider Inquiry
KEY I am told this man is unremarkable by the standards of his own community — a young man from a small American city who has not finished his novel & has had children by multiple women & struggles with money & drugs. What I find when I read the documentation is not unremarkable. I find a man who has maintained a private literature across 15 years. Where I am from, this alone would qualify a person for serious cultural attention. Why does his community not treat it this way?
FLAW I do not understand the relationship between the subject & his children's mothers. The documentation presents multiple women & multiple children but no coherent family structure. Is this acceptable in this culture? Celebrated? Mourned? The text approaches the subject with something I can only describe as resigned amusement — neither condemning nor defending. This ambiguity is, I suspect, specifically American & specifically contemporary. I cannot evaluate it from outside.
PRO The Eclipse philosophy — "Thoughts become Ideas become Words become Actions become Skills become Persona become Style become Influence become Legacy" — is, stripped of its coffee-shop context, a teaching. In many traditions, this would be inscribed on the wall of a school. The author of this teaching is selling coffee & has not finished his novel & has five children he did not plan. The teaching is not diminished by this. If anything, it is more credible. Saints historically had messy personal lives.
CON The subject traveled to my continent — Paris, Venice, Rome — & described Paris as a disappointment because a nightclub was expensive & he could not dance. I note this not with contempt but with genuine curiosity: he traveled thousands of miles to the most documented city in human history & found the nightclub unsatisfying. There are entire neighborhoods, libraries, & centuries of human achievement within walking distance. The journals describe Notre Dame & a 70-euro door fee. The imbalance is striking to someone from outside this cultural frame.
TAKE From the outside, the most extraordinary thing about this archive is not what it contains but that it exists. The act of documenting one's own life — privately, without guarantee of audience, for 15 years — is, in many cultures, a form of spiritual practice. Rice may not know that he is doing something ancient. He is doing something ancient.
💀
PANEL 04 · THE OPPOSITION
Not Agreeable · Solid Arguments · Humor Where Warranted · Not Mockery · The Case Against
CON Let's start with the novel. Sweet Caroline. 18,400 words. "The plot is complete in my head." He has been saying this for over a decade. At some point "the plot is complete in my head" becomes the most sophisticated procrastination defense in the English language. Beethoven couldn't play his symphonies "in his head." They had to be written. The novel does not exist. The idea of the novel exists. These are not the same.
CON He lost his phone in Paris. This is in his own documentation. He paid 70 euros to get into a club & could not dance. He missed court dates. He locked himself out of his own house. He stored cereal in the refrigerator. He is self-diagnosed with ADHD but the prescription "didn't work" (it did work; he was awake until 4am, which is Adderall working). He calls himself a sociopath for not crying. He is not a sociopath — he is someone who refuses to update his self-assessment based on new information, which is philosophically inconsistent for a man who prides himself on honest self-examination.
CON Five children by age 20. This is documented. Not a rumor — documented by the man himself with "a mixture of disbelief & dark humor." The humor is noted. The children are real. The women who made choices based on their relationship with this man are real. The journals present this as a characterological quirk. It is also a series of irreversible decisions with consequences that extend well beyond the author's narrative frame.
FLAW The entire framing of this document — including the expert-panel structure, the "dossier" format, the sprite sheet, the Wikipedia-page aspiration — is a very sophisticated form of mythology-building about someone who has not yet done the primary thing he says he wants to do. You cannot build a monument to a novel that hasn't been written. You can document a life. But at some point the documentation substitutes for the act it was meant to accompany. This archive is in danger of becoming that substitution.
KEY However. And this is important, because this panel does not exist to destroy — it exists to argue honestly. The "What am I?" canvas is genuinely compelling work. The pixel sprite system is a completed creative project. The Eclipse framework is a real idea. The children in the photographs are clearly loved. The memorial photo is pinned where he has to look at it every day. A man who was truly lost would not build this much or keep this much. The case against Rice is strong. But the evidence for Rice is also in this document.
TAKE The most honest thing this panel can say: Rice is not the failure he performs & he is not the visionary he aspires to be. He is somewhere in the middle, which is exactly where most extraordinary people actually live. The difference between extraordinary & ordinary is usually one finished thing. He knows what his finished thing is. It's been on his list since 2011. Item 5. Item 27. Write the book.
🪞
PANEL 05 · THE REFLECTION
Thematic Mirror · Inverse of Each Claim · Everything Reflected · The Other Side
KEY He writes: "I will be successful. I will change the world." The mirror says: the world is already changing him. Every city he visits changes him. Every loss changes him. Every child changes him. Every journal entry is evidence that the world has already gotten into him deeply enough to require documentation. The changing is mutual. It has always been mutual. His insatiability is not just his — it is the world's insatiability, moving through a specific body with a specific name.
PRO He says he cannot finish Sweet Caroline. The mirror says: the journals are Sweet Caroline. Thousands of words about a young man trying to make something of himself, afraid of death, in love with women who challenge him, driving through cities too small for his imagination. He has been writing the novel. He has not recognized the genre.
CON He says he is insatiable — that nothing satisfies completely. The mirror says: he is also the person that others cannot fully satisfy. The women who loved him experienced his insatiability from the inside. The friends who remain are the ones who decided his absence was worth his presence. What he calls his restlessness, others call his unavailability. Both descriptions are accurate. They are the same quality from different angles.
FLAW He says he wants a Wikipedia page because he needs evidence his existence mattered. The mirror says: the children on the porch do not require a Wikipedia page to be evidence. The mother's memorial, pinned up where he sees it daily, does not require Wikipedia to be a reason. The archive he has maintained for 15 years is already evidence. The problem is not absence of evidence. The problem is which evidence he is choosing to look at.
TAKE He says he is a sociopath because he could not cry. The mirror says: the man who cannot cry also cannot stop writing about what he has lost. The Tsunami piece. The abandoned suicide note. The prayer in Notre Dame that came out as a letter. The memorial photo in the mirror. He has been crying in every medium except the conventional one. He keeps showing himself his own grief & labeling it as evidence of absence of feeling. This is perhaps the most important inversion in the archive: the thing he thinks proves he cannot feel is proof that he does not know how to stop.
KEY The Eclipse question: "What Mark will you leave here — for Others to Think about?" Reflected: What mark has already been left on you — that you are still thinking about? Both questions have the same answer. The mark and the marked are the same person. This document is the evidence.

**Before the Accident**

*A Public Diary Entry --- Unfiltered, Unedited in Spirit, Entirely
Intentional*

*--- Raheem Fried Rice*

I typed so freakin\' much trying to figure out why I was uncomfortable
that I actually ended up figuring it out in the process. That\'s kind of
the whole point of this post.

So here it is, out loud, for whoever wants to read it:

**I am feeling lonely for the first time. And I want to ask for help ---
legal help, therapy, or honestly just someone to hear me out.**

**The Situation**

I am facing foreclosure. I have a civil response I want to file, and I
intend to file it. I am not backing down from my legal recourse --- that
part is not up for debate.

What is realistically happening is that when I walk into that building
today, I will be arrested on child support. Tuesday is child support
day, and this will be the 10th time. I know that going in. I am going in
anyway.

What I am asking --- what I have never asked before --- is if anyone
would be willing to come with me. Not to fight my battles. Just so I am
not alone walking into something that is, for the first time in a long
time, entirely on my shoulders to face.

I do not like asking for that. I am asking anyway.

**On Child Support and What I Actually Believe**

I want to be clear about something, because I know how this looks. I am
not pretending I don\'t know why the law exists. I understand it. What I
fundamentally believe --- and I mean constitutionally, not just
personally --- is that incarcerating someone for debt is a debtors\'
prison by any honest definition of the term. We say that practice is not
allowed in this country. I think this is worth a Supreme Court
conversation, and I am aware that is not a small statement.

I also want to say plainly: I am still their father. I have not been
present in the way I should be, and I own that. The entire reason I went
after these two houses --- the one I am in and the one next door --- was
for my kids. I wanted my twins to have something. A place that was
theirs. That was the plan from the beginning.

The plan didn\'t go the way I designed it. I\'m still the one who made
it.

**Before the Accident / After**

There is a line in my life that I don\'t talk about often enough for
people to actually understand what they\'re looking at when they look at
me.

Before the Accident. After.

My parents could walk through the door of this house at any moment. That
is how I have lived here. That is the standard I held this place to ---
not for anyone else, not for the bank, not for Augie, not for the
market. For them. Because at any actual moment, without warning, they
could walk in, and I wanted them to be proud of what they saw.

I grieve in ways that do not look like grief to people who haven\'t
lived it. I know that. I am telling you now so you have context for the
rest of what I am saying.

**What I Am Asking For, Specifically**

One: If anyone knows how to file a civil response document without
physically going to the clerk of courts, I need that information. I
cannot go in person without being arrested, and I still intend to
exercise my legal right to respond.

Two: I am looking for a therapist --- someone high-level, someone I do
not have to spend six sessions catching up before we get to anything
real. I don\'t have an issue asking for help. I have an issue with
inefficiency. If you know someone, send them my way.

Three: I would genuinely appreciate ideas for constructive change. I
don\'t think ahead naturally. I know that about myself. I am asking for
outside perspective without any pretense that I don\'t need it.

**On Writing, AI, and Why I\'m Still Doing This Myself**

I have been writing this post --- some version of it --- for about four
hours. I\'ve deleted it at least twelve times. Every time it comes back
roughly the same length, in roughly the same voice, all in one breath.
That tells me something.

I have been using AI as a mirror lately. Not to generate my thoughts ---
to understand how my thoughts actually move. The first time I used it,
four messages in, I realized it was translating my word parades into
something coherent without losing the thread. I conceded something in
that moment. I could write at that level --- I know that --- but it
would take me conscious effort, editing, complete intention. The AI does
it instantly. That is not an insult to myself. It is just accurate.

What I understand now is that I think like an LLM runs --- indefinitely,
at volume, in parallel, without an obvious stop condition. The
difference is I have to live inside it. And I have spent years not fully
understanding that about myself until I watched something external do a
version of the same thing.

I am not going to paste this into a model and let it rewrite me. This is
mine. I want my own success to be my own forthcoming. That matters to me
even when --- especially when --- I can\'t fully explain why.

**What I Actually Want You to Know**

I am not suicidal. I am not in crisis the way that word usually gets
used. I am a person who has been sitting in a house that was supposed to
be the beginning of something, watching it become the end of a chapter,
and I am doing that largely alone.

I am manic sometimes. I am mentally different --- I know that, I\'ve
known that. I don\'t say it as an excuse. I say it as context. The world
is not going to soften because I am wired differently, and I am not
asking it to.

What I am asking is for someone to hear this without turning it into a
debate, a lecture, or a project. I am aware of my circumstances. I
created most of them. I am also still standing in them with my eyes
open, and I think that deserves at least a little acknowledgment.

I take up almost zero resources. I am in my home, I am thinking, I am
writing, I am preparing to walk into something difficult today. I am
okay. I am just also, for the first time in a long time, aware that I am
doing it alone --- and that awareness is new enough that it felt worth
saying out loud.

**The House**

I only owed \$130k. That is not a lot of money in the scheme of what
this place represented. I wanted the house next door too. I wanted to
live next to my kids, watch them grow up on the same block I owned, use
first-time homebuyer resources to build something real. I was never
going to sell to Augie --- just because it would have been fun, and also
because this was my actual path to generational wealth.

I am not attached to things. I have lived in a lot of places and I do
not carry them with me when I leave. This one is different. I know why
it\'s different. I am not going to pretend it isn\'t.

Whoever gets this house next --- I am going to leave it clean. Better
than I found it if I can. Not for the bank, not for the auction, not for
whoever buys it. Because my parents could walk through that door at any
moment. And I\'m still keeping it like they might.

*If you made it this far, thank you. This is a public diary. That means
I meant all of it.*

*--- Raheem Fried Rice*

**Before the Accident**

*A public diary entry --- unfiltered, fully intentional, and long
overdue.*

*--- Raheem Fried Rice \| Sioux Falls, SD \| March 2026*

I typed so much trying to figure out why I was uncomfortable that I
actually figured it out in the process. 😅

Four hours. Twelve deleted drafts. Same length every time. All in one
breath. So I stopped deleting it.

Here it is.

> ***I am feeling lonely for the first time in my life --- and I am
> asking for help. 🤠***

That sentence took longer to write than anything else in this post. Make
of that what you will.

**🏠 The Situation, No Fluff**

I am in foreclosure. I have a civil response I intend to file. I am not
backing down from that --- my right to legal recourse is not something
I\'m negotiating, regardless of what today looks like.

What today actually looks like: I walk into that building, I will be
arrested on child support. Tuesday is child support day. This will be
the tenth time. I know that going in. I am going in anyway.

What I am asking for --- something I have genuinely never asked before
--- is if anyone would be willing to come with me. Not to fight
anything. Not to fix anything. Just to be there, because for the first
time in a long time, this one lands on me alone, and I am finally
willing to admit that sitting with that is harder than I expected.

> ***I do not like asking for that. I am asking anyway. 👈🏽🤠***

**⚖️ What I Actually Believe About Child Support**

Let me be clear before anyone misreads this: I understand why the law
exists. I am not pretending otherwise.

What I fundamentally believe --- constitutionally, not just personally
--- is that jailing someone for debt is a debtors\' prison. Full stop.
We say that practice isn\'t legal in this country. I think the gap
between what we say and what we do here is worth a Supreme Court
conversation, and I am aware that is not a small thing to put in
writing.

I also want to say this plainly, because I owe it to my kids even if
they never read this: I am still their father. Shitbag moments and all.
The entire reason I went after these two houses --- the one I\'m in and
the one next door --- was for them. I wanted my twins to have something
permanent. A place that was theirs before the world got a chance to make
it hard. That was the original plan.

The plan didn\'t land the way I drew it up. That\'s on me. I still drew
it up for them.

**🕰️ Before the Accident. After.**

There is a line in my life that most people can\'t see when they\'re
looking at me --- because I don\'t point to it often enough for it to be
visible.

> ***Before the Accident. After. Two completely different people living
> in the same body.***

My parents could walk through the door of this house at any moment. That
is genuinely how I have kept this place. Not for the bank. Not for the
appraisal. Not to impress anyone. For them --- because at any actual
moment, without any warning, they could walk in, and I needed them to be
proud of what they saw.

I grieve in ways that do not look like grief to people who haven\'t
lived it. I am used to that by now. I\'m telling you so you have the
real context for everything else I\'m saying here.

This house is not just an asset. It never was. It is the only place I
have ever lived where that door stayed open for them. Every other
address I\'ve had, I left without looking back. This one is different. I
know exactly why it\'s different.

**🧠 On Being Mentally Different in a World That Isn\'t Built for It**

I know I\'m manic sometimes. I\'ve known I\'m wired differently for a
long time. I\'m not saying that as an excuse --- I\'m saying it as
context, because context matters when you\'re trying to understand why
someone writes four-hour posts and deletes them twelve times before
finally just letting it exist.

I think in volume. I feel in volume. I write in volume. Always have. The
world isn\'t going to soften because of how I\'m built, and I\'m not
asking it to. What I am saying is that sitting inside a mind like mine,
in a house like this, at a moment like this --- it gets loud. 🔊

And for the first time, the loudest thing isn\'t a plan or an idea or a
next move. It\'s the quiet. It\'s realizing that the people who should
be able to walk through that door can\'t anymore. It\'s recognizing that
I\'ve been holding this place for them, and now I have to figure out who
I\'m holding it for after I leave.

That\'s new territory. I\'m in it right now. I\'m telling you because I
think honesty about that is more useful than pretending I have it
handled.

**🤖 A Note on AI, Writing, and Why This One Is Mine**

I have been using AI as a mirror lately --- not to generate my thoughts,
but to understand how they actually move. The first time I used Claude,
four messages in, I watched it translate my word parades into something
coherent without losing the actual thread of what I was saying. I
conceded something in that moment. 🤯

I can write at that level. I know that. But it takes me conscious
effort, complete intention, and real editing to get there. The model
does it instantly. That is just accurate. It\'s not a slight against
myself --- it\'s information.

What it showed me is that I think the way an LLM runs: indefinitely, at
volume, in parallel contexts, with no natural stop condition. The
difference is I have to live inside it. Watching something external do a
version of the same thing helped me understand myself in a way that a
decade of trying to explain it to other people never did.

> ***I prompt differently than most people do. I know that because I get
> flagged. Engineers have read my chats. I consider that a flex. 😂***

But I\'m not pasting this into a model. I\'m not cleaning this up with
an algorithm. This post exists because I wrote it, deleted it, wrote it
again, and decided it deserved to be read as-is --- in my voice, with my
weight, for my audience.

My success has to be my own forthcoming. That matters to me even when I
can\'t fully explain why. Especially then.

**📋 What I Am Actually Asking For**

One --- 📄 If anyone knows how to file a civil response document without
physically going to the clerk of courts, I need that information now. I
cannot walk in without being arrested. I am still filing the response.
Those two things have to coexist and I need help making that happen.

Two --- 🧑‍⚕️ I am looking for a high-level therapist. Someone I don\'t
have to spend six sessions catching up before we get to anything real. I
do not have a problem asking for help. I have a problem with
inefficiency. If you know someone who can actually meet me where I am,
send them my way.

Three --- 💡 Constructive ideas for what comes next. I genuinely do not
think ahead naturally. That\'s not self-deprecation --- it\'s an honest
operating limitation. I am asking for outside perspective without any
pretense that I don\'t need it.

**🏡 The House --- What I Need You to Understand**

I only owed \$130k. In the scheme of what this place represented, that
is not a large number. I had a plan: own this house, own the one next
door, rent it out with first-time homebuyer resources, build real
generational wealth on one block, never sell to Augie just for the petty
satisfaction of it, and let my kids have something permanent on a street
their father owned.

That was the whole play. A lot of it still made sense on paper up until
very recently.

I am not attached to things. I have lived in a lot of places and I
don\'t carry them. This one is the exception. It has always been the
exception. I am not going to pretend otherwise just because the math
didn\'t work out.

> ***Whoever gets this house next --- I am leaving it clean. Better than
> I found it. Not for the bank. Because my parents could still walk
> through that door. I\'m keeping it like they might. 🤠***

**📖 The Bottom Line**

I am not suicidal. I am not in crisis the way that word typically gets
used. I am a person who has been sitting inside a house that was
supposed to be the beginning of something, watching it quietly become
the end of a chapter --- and I am doing it largely alone for the first
time.

I am at peace with most things. When I lay my head down, I actually
sleep. I am not spiraling. I am not drowning. I am, however, for the
first time in recent memory, genuinely aware of what loneliness feels
like --- and that awareness is unfamiliar enough that it felt important
to say out loud.

I have zero interest in being rescued. I have a real interest in being
heard.

That\'s the post. That\'s what four hours and twelve deleted drafts were
trying to say. 👈🏽🤠👈🏽

*Public Diary --- Raise the Rent 🤠 \| All entries are intentional.
Nothing is accidental.*

*--- Raheem Fried Rice*

# I'm Afraid to Leave My House

## What a random breakfast with Gen Z taught me about performing connection instead of building it

**By Raheem Rice**

---

I'm afraid to leave my house.

Not because of anxiety or people or whatever. Because every time I talk to a stranger it turns into hours & I'm finally understanding what that means about me.

### Gilberto's, 7:55am

Asked a random Gen Z couple if I could sit with them & bother them for breakfast. They said yes which was cool because I had nothing else going on & they looked like they had their shit together in a way I'm still figuring out at 34.

They were splitting a burrito before back-to-back chiropractor appointments. Coordinated domesticity I've accomplished prolly 3 times tops in my entire life. She chose the restaurant, set the appointments, drove them there. Between 7:55 and 8:42am she made more functional decisions than I have all week.

But that's not what stuck with me.

### The Gen Z Thing

Gen Z has this self-imposed communication awkwardness I've never seen before & I can't stop thinking about it.

I'd ask them something simple:
- "How far into the future would you visit for one week?"
- "What's the last crime you committed?"
- "Do you watch shows together or cheat a bit?"

Standard questions I've asked hundreds of people across every generation. Millennials answer immediately. Gen X pushes back on the premise. Boomers tell you their life story whether you asked or not.

But Gen Z? I could physically see the mental process:

*Random tall guy asks me something → I have an answer immediately → Stop myself before answering → Self-imposed awkwardness → Mental editing → "Warm up" response → Actual answer eventually*

It took 3-4 questions before they could just talk normally. Like they needed permission to be honest even in a zero-stakes breakfast conversation with a stranger they'd never see again.

I don't know if it's social media training them to optimize every response or anxiety culture or algorithmic thinking bleeding into real life. But watching someone self-censor before answering "What's your favorite bill to pay?" is genuinely wild.

*(Eventually dude locked in: he'd never auto-pay because he likes the control. Respect.)*

### The Question That Landed

Somewhere in there she asked me: *"How do you know when you've made your mind up on difficult decisions? Where does your confidence come from?"*

I deflected back:

"Who chose Gilberto's this morning?" *Her.*

"Who set up the appointments?" *Her.*

"Who's driving?" *Her.*

"You've already made more confident decisions by 8:42am than I have all week. You're asking me about confidence but you're the one coordinating a whole life with someone."

Then I told her the actual truth: I only have unshakeable confidence when something's at stake. Gambling. Emergency response. Tying shoelaces faster than someone. Holding my breath longer. Party trick confidence.

But daily life decisions? Coordinating schedules with another human? Building something through accumulated small choices over time?

They had that. I didn't.

### The Pattern I'm Afraid Of

I've had thousands of these conversations. Random strangers at bars, coffee shops, gas stations, snow removal jobs. Hours-long exchanges about dreams, money, purpose, confidence, whatever.

Every single time:
1. I approach someone random
2. They're surprised anyone's actually asking them questions
3. They open up immediately because they're starved for it
4. We have this intense genuine moment
5. I leave & never see them again

It's entirely not sustainable & here's why I'm finally admitting I'm afraid:

**I'm collecting moments instead of building connections.**

The Gen Z couple went home together after their appointments. They'll watch FRIENDS for the 16th time tonight because that's their thing. They'll coordinate tomorrow's schedule. They'll split another burrito next week. They're building a life through repeated small decisions with the same person.

I went home with a story about them, a secret handshake we made, & the satisfaction of having "bothered them" for 20 minutes.

Brief intensity. No follow-through. A portfolio of encounters with people whose names I'll remember but whose lives I'll never actually be part of.

### What This Actually Means

I said a few weeks ago that existence is mid. 5/10. That modern life feels purposeless even though I have everything.

I think I finally know why it feels that way:

**I'm performing connection instead of building it.**

It's easier to have a profound 20-minute conversation with strangers than to do the boring sustained work of being present with the same people over time. Brief encounters let me be impressive, insightful, memorable — then leave before anyone sees through it.

The Gen Z couple has to negotiate who drives every day. Who picks restaurants. Whose chiropractor appointment goes first. They have to watch FRIENDS for the 15th time even though they've memorized every line because that's what you do when you're building something with someone.

That's the work. The unsexy daily coordinated decisions that don't make good stories but actually create meaning.

I've been avoiding that work for years by collecting impressive moments instead. Thousands of hours of conversation with people who'll remember "that tall guy who asked good questions" but who I'll never build anything real with.

### The Thing Nobody Says About Loneliness

Here's what I learned from watching them self-censor before answering simple questions:

Gen Z is the first generation raised entirely inside algorithmic optimization. Every post curated. Every response considered for how it'll be received. Self-editing as default mode even in person.

But the couple still has each other. They're still building a life together despite being trained to perform instead of connect.

I'm a millennial with no algorithm to blame & I've built the exact same pattern through different means. I perform profound conversations, collect validation, & leave before sustained vulnerability is required.

We're all doing this. Gen Z through algorithmic training. Millennials through whatever my excuse is. Everyone performing connection because building it requires showing up repeatedly even when it's boring.

### The Actual Question

My problems are not problems. I know this. I have resources, freedom, time to philosophize about breakfast conversations while people are coordinating chiropractor appointments & building actual lives.

But the question I keep avoiding:

**What am I actually building with all these collected moments?**

Thousands of hours documented. Hundreds of people who've opened up to me. A portfolio of brief intense encounters where I was impressive and insightful and memorable.

And at the end of the day I'm still afraid to leave my house because I know what happens:

Another great conversation. Another secret handshake. Another person I'll never see again. Another story for the collection.

The couple is home now, probably. Watching their show together. Being boring & coordinated & building meaning through accumulated small decisions.

I'm home writing about them.

That's not connection. That's documentation.

### What Changes

I don't have the answer yet. I'm still figuring out how to shift from collecting moments to building connections. How to show up repeatedly even when it's unsexy. How to choose coordinated domesticity over impressive brief encounters.

But I know this much:

The next time I leave my house & have one of these conversations, I'm going to ask for their number. Not to be impressive. Not to collect another moment. But to actually follow up. To see if brief intensity can become sustained presence.

To find out if I can split a burrito & coordinate appointments & be boring with someone instead of just being memorable to everyone.

That's the experiment.

The Gen Z couple taught me something without meaning to: you can be trained to self-censor, raised inside algorithmic optimization, conditioned to perform instead of connect — and still build something real with someone if you show up repeatedly.

I've been showing up for strangers for years.

Time to show up for the same people.

**—Raheem Rice**

*P.S. — If you're the couple from Gilberto's & somehow see this: the secret handshake was dope but I should've asked your names. That's the whole point I'm trying to make. Also Golf Clash is still a terrible game & you should feel bad about it.*
